heaviest_rainfall.status: Today ranked highest for the heaviest total rainfall during a storm, dropping a total of 47.89mm of rain over the storm duration of 144.8 hours with a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: Today, the rain fell at an intense rate peaking at 24.6mm per hour, one of the heaviest downpours recorded and ranked 2nd from our records.  The precipitation lasted 144.8 hours and dumped 47.89mm in total.  The heaviest rain was recorded on 02 September 2025 with a peak rate of 34.3mm per hour.  The storm lasted 15.0 hours and had a total rainfall of 21.19mm.

longest_rain.status: Rain fell for 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 (ranking highest from our records for the longest storm) and it produced a total of 47.89mm of rain at a maximum intensity of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The highest recorded number of lightning strikes in a single day is {highest_count} on {highest_date} since records began on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: A storm is in the area.  This is the first such event since 08 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The ongoing rainy stretch, 9 days and counting started on 07 November 2025 and ranks 8th in length since records started in 23 September 2024.  The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is no cold snap at the moment.  This year has experienced 6 cold snaps, with an average of 3.7 per year.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 26.7°C from 29 April 2025 (when the temperature reached 27.1°C but fell to 0.4°C).

heatwaves.status: At the moment, it is not a heatwaves.  This year has seen 6 heatwavess; typically, 1.9 heatwavess happen every year.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was 18 July 2025, there have been only 62 tropical nights since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (182°), which differs from the daily average south-westerly (229.0°) by approximately 47.0 degrees.    In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The highest wind gust ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: The record wind run remains at 1737 miles from 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The highest wind speed ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 2.5°C) and outside temperature (down 1.6°C) and drier conditions in rainfall (down 0.2 mm).

The past week and month had observable variation in rainfall (up 0.1 mm) and cloud levels (up 269 m) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 136.3m higher than yesterday, 19.8m lower than the past week, 268.9m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 561m minimum and 715m maximum, with an average of 747.9m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 767.7m and 1016.8m.
cloud levels.summary: Expect overcast skies with low cloud.

summary.summary: Inside; a mild and comfortable temperature is anticipated. Outside; mild and pleasant. Also, air will feel comfortable and balanced and mostly calm with light air movement. A few light raindrops may fall, overcast skies with low cloud, and bright intervals likely. Weather could become unsettled.

washing.phrase: The humidity is too high (94%) for your washing to dry quickly.  Any drying will take longer than usual.
